page: Microsoft Azure D14_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 112.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 819 GB, while no dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by average ratings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory read bandwidth is average, though large-block write bandwidth is weak. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware flags such as AES, AVX, BMI2, and FMA.

The D14_v2 offers a high memory-to-core ratio and substantial local storage, making it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring large memory footprints without demanding top-tier single-threaded CPU speed. Tradeoffs include poor single-threaded compression and slow text generation in LLM inference. This server is best suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and memory-intensive general-purpose workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D14_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 112.0 GB of system memory (7.0 GB per core) and 819 GB of local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and large-block memory writes are weak, whereas multi-core operations, database transactions, and static web serving achieve average tier performance. This resource profile makes the D14_v2 qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy, multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suitable for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose applications that benefit from a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage capacity without requiring high single-threaded compute performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D14_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine featuring 16 Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 cores, 112 GB of RAM, and 819 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D14_v2 is a Hyper-V virtual machine with 16 Intel Xeon cores, 112 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A memory-rich virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and substantial local storage for balanced multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 cores running at 2.3 GHz
112.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core
819 GB of bundled local storage capacity
Average multi-core and database performance with weaker single-core execution
Well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing
